plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and meet mandate.
Sell SOXL and SPAL to eliminate all leveraged ETF exposure.
Reduce QQQI and QQQ due to downtrends and to free up capital.
Add MSFT, a high-quality name, to diversify and improve overall quality.
This plan aims for capital preservation and reduces overall risk significantly.
Maintain a substantial cash buffer of 15-20% after these moves.
conservative.read: This conservative approach aims for a modest ~1.5% per month, prioritizing capital preservation. Risk is significantly reduced by eliminating leveraged ETFs and lowering concentration, leading to lower potential drawdown.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to align with the concentration mandate.
Reduce SOXL to a small 3% and sell SPAL to lower overall leverage.
Trim QQQI and QQQ due to their downtrends, reallocating capital.
Buy NVDU and MSFT, strong scanner setups, to add quality tech exposure.
This approach balances growth with risk management, maintaining a 10% cash buffer.
Total adds will not exceed total trims plus the cash buffer.
balanced.read: This balanced approach aims for ~2.5% per month, focusing on risk-first growth. It reduces leverage and concentration while introducing quality tech, balancing upside potential with controlled drawdown.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate.
Increase SOXL exposure to 10% to lean into leveraged semiconductor upside.
Sell SPAL to remove an underperforming leveraged ETF.
Trim QQQI and QQQ significantly to free up capital for higher-conviction plays.
Buy NVDU and MSFT, top scanner setups, for concentrated growth.
This plan uses leverage and concentration for aggressive growth, with a 5% cash buffer.
aggressive.read: This aggressive approach targets ~4% per month, embracing higher risk for stretched growth. It involves increased leverage and concentration, which amplifies both potential gains and drawdowns. Be aware that leveraged ETFs like SOXL experience daily decay and rebalancing, requiring strict stop discipline.

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and free capital for income.
Sell SOXL and SPAL to eliminate all leveraged and high-beta exposure.
Increase QQQI to 25% for enhanced monthly distributions.
Reduce QQQ, SMH, and CRWD to lower overall growth-oriented exposure.
This plan prioritizes steady monthly distributions and low drawdown.
Maintain a significant cash buffer to support income stability.
income.read: This income approach targets ~1% per month, focusing on yield and stability. It prioritizes income ETFs and broad index exposure, aiming for low drawdown. Distributions are variable and NAV can still fall in selloffs; this is not a savings account.
